programme.desc: A Russian immigrant stockbroker is found dead and a strange phone call to his office is the only clue investigators have to go on - until they're led to a client who's made some bad investments, and a dog that sheds a little more than his owner realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Just Desserts
programme.desc: A Florida couple is found dead just days before their planned move out of state. A known criminal's driver's license found at the crime scene seems too good to be true.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Driven to Silence
programme.desc: An attractive young hairstylist is murdered in her South Carolina salon, and police are at a loss. A tiny hair and a DNA test force police to take another look nearly a decade after the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Printed Proof
programme.desc: The murders of two publicly known gay activists led investigators to believe they were victims of a hate-crime. The police soon were able to uncover the real motive behind the murders, and it wasn't hate.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Capitol Crimes
programme.desc: Police suspect a man in a hit and run, but he leased the car in question to someone in another state. Police found the vehicle and took it apart, uncovering evidence telling them just what happened.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Undertaken
programme.desc: The medical examiner ruled the death an accident, but the detectives investigating the case thought the evidence at the scene indicated otherwise. It would take three years, an exhumation and a second autopsy to determine who was right.

programme.title: Death Row Stories: The Wrong Gun
programme.desc: In Texas, a 17-year-old with a violent past is named the killer in a drug deal gone wrong - but a misidentified bullet could unravel the case.

programme.title: Golf
programme.desc: Golf.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Locomotive Builder
programme.desc: Mike heads to MotivePower in Boise, Idaho to build and restore locomotives for his 200th dirty job.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Wetland Warrior
programme.desc: Mike travels to Texas to work as a stand-in fugitive while attempting to outsmart a team of bloodhounds and evade being captured. Then he heads to the Florida Everglades to help combat non-native, invasive species in the marshlands.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Rum Distiller
programme.desc: Mike heads to Newport Distillery to help the crew turn molasses into rum. Scouring copper, working with scalding liquid, hauling hoses and lugging molasses cubes are all just part of a hard day's work.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Termite Researcher
programme.desc: Mike visits with long-time fans Marilyn and Carolyn Maedel to look at never-before-seen footage at a crawfish factory in Louisiana. Then, he experiences the many hands-on duties that come with being a termite researcher on Sapolo Island in Georgia.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Vellum Maker
programme.desc: Mike travels to Yakima, Washington to harvest hops, the main ingredient in beer. From there Mike goes to the Hudson Valley where he turns animal hides in to paper.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Gourd Maker
programme.desc: Mike travels to the middle of Connecticut to clean out a lake, and then drops by a gourd farm in Pennsylvania and transforms gourds into a variety of products.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Exotic Nanny
programme.desc: Mike travels to Texas to help out at a ranch that harbours and breeds different exotic animal species including lemurs, sloths, bearcats and camels.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Lightning Rod Installer
programme.desc: Mike travels to Lucedale, Mississippi to try his hand at installing a lightning rod system on a home.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Sea Lamprey Exterminator
programme.desc: In Michigan, Mike helps the U.S. Department of Fish & Wildlife control the sea lamprey population that is jeopardizing the fishing industry of the Great Lakes. Then at Mandalay Bay Casino Mike makes 'meat gel' to feed the fish in the shark reef aquarium.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Blueberry Connoisseur
programme.desc: Mike creates an 'interesting' flavour at the Jelly Belly Candy Company.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Greenland Shark Quest

programme.title: Smalltown Throwdown: The Most Boring and The Drunkest

programme.title: Destroyed in Seconds

programme.title: Jimmy Houston Outdoors: Pow Stick!

programme.title: Into the Blue: Tuna Time

programme.title: Sportfishing Adventures: Snookin' Around Jupiter

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A survival instructor and retiree of the armed forces are inserted into the volcano-ravaged island of Montserrat. With completely different survival styles, they find a way to work together, but one accident single-handedly has an eruptive effect on both.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Stomping Grounds
programme.desc: Elite survivalists Matt Wright and Gary Golding are tested by deadly spitting cobras and territorial elephants in an African killing zone. But Matt and Gary aren't alone each must protect a novice survivalist from the threats surrounding them.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Shaken and Very Stirred
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake in the Philippine jungle rattles a thrill-seeking chef and a competitive hockey mom but lack of food and severe dehydration lead to a disastrous face-first fall. Later, two superfans are dropped deep in the Amazon rainforest.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Two strangers are dropped in one of the most dangerous African locations in the dead heat of Summer. Faced with aggressive hippos and charging elephants they quickly learn their place, but must mark their territory if they want to make it twenty-one days.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Baked Alaskan
programme.desc: A deadly spider bite puts Gwen, an Alaskan homesteader at risk of tapping but she works with her partner to get her revenge, in the 130-degree heat of the Mexican jungle.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Countdown to XL: Second Time's the Charm
programme.desc: Former survivalists re-visit their pioneering 21-day challenge in Ecuador as a threesome. They ventured into the jungle, facing jaguars along the way, as well as a two-on-one dynamic. Now, they must prepare for a 40-day challenge in Africa.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Countdown to XL: Only in Brazil
programme.desc: A wilderness educator and IT business owner reflect on their 21-day challenge in the Brazilian wild, including a heart-stopping encounter with a jaguar. Their experiences have prepared them well for their upcoming 40-day challenge in Africa.

programme.desc: Arline's hoard has reached such immense proportions that her husband has been sleeping in his car each night to recover from surgery. Carolyn's family will press charges if she doesn't return stolen items.

programme.desc: When a Cleveland man is shot while driving his car, detectives search four states to find his killer. In Miami, a man is gunned down, but detectives have to rely on tight-lipped citizens to solve the murder.

programme.title: The First 48: Night Shift/ Mobbed

programme.title: The First 48: Kiss of Death
programme.desc: In Rochester, a man from a neighboring suburb is found shot to death on a city street.

programme.title: Tirdy Works: What Matters Most
programme.desc: Mary attempts to diversify into other types of tird products, and is invited to present as part of Career Day at Katie's high school.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Three Men and Your Baby
programme.desc: The styles of dance Murr claims to be skilled in. The Joker that recently became a published author.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Q-Pay
programme.desc: Find out which joker has a weak spot for animals that the other guys love to exploit, who made the best wingman back in his single days and how long it took Q to grow out his hair long enough for a wig.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Up Loser's Creek
programme.desc: Find out which joker ran for Congress as a write-in candidate, which joker was a top-notch salesman before starting the show and who had a job in finance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 1
programme.desc: Be a guest at a dinner party with the stars of the show. Join Sal, Joe, Q and Murr aka 'The Tenderloins,' as they dish about their week, their friendship and whatever else is thrown on their dinner table.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Flatfoot the Pirate
programme.desc: Who made the call to give Murr a face full of slushie at the mall? How did the Jokers keep their props secret from each other before Laugh Man Standing? And whose breast milk did Pirate Sal chug?

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Lost Boy
programme.desc: Find out which joker once worked as a fish monger, who lied on his resume to snag a TV internship and what chain restaurant the guys used during Sal's 'shush!' punishment at the mall.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Take Me Out at the Ballgame
programme.desc: Find out how Murr chose which guys to honk at Volcano Bay, what happened to the TP used in the stock boy shootout, and how Joe stayed safe after messing with NY Mets fans at Citi Field.

programme.title: Movie: I Now Pronounce You Chuck and Larry
programme.desc: A firefighter agrees to enter a same-sex marriage with one of his colleagues so he can name his children as his life insurance beneficiaries. The media soon turn the blessed event into headline news.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Lucy Wants a Friend
programme.desc: Lucy convinces Chief McConky to transfer Delilah Dunn to the station, which causes the men in the team to seek a macho match to their energy. Meanwhile, Eddie takes Councilwoman Price for a walk on the wild side.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Snow Way Out
programme.desc: Find out which Joker only drinks coffee after 2pm; who is most self-conscious about singing; and how long did Joe and Q wait inside while Murr and Sal hung out on a ski lift in the freezing cold.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Sneaking Number Twos, Going Number One
programme.desc: Find out why the jokers love filming at the grocery store, how many pencils were purchased for put-pocketing and why Sal's pee punishment was twice as terrible as it looked.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Car Sick
programme.desc: Find out what celebrity Sal wrote fan letters to as a kid, which grocery game is Murr's favourite, how the guys found sloppy Joe and who helped Q's first jeep punishment a milky mess.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Guilty as Charged
programme.desc: Find out why Q messed with a fishmonger at Fairway instead of a shopper, what nearly caused Sal to lose it during his UFO presentation and how the crew nabbed close ups from inside the mob during Q's punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Good Deed
programme.desc: Find out what after-school activity Joe's mom forbid him from doing, why filming nearly halted during the jack asses challenge and what last minute idea made it into Sal and Joe's charity funds punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Indecent Proposal
programme.desc: Find out why Murr had to shave in under three minutes between scenes of That Ain't Me, what the Jokers love most about Writers Blocked, and where Sal ranks his indecent dance instructor punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Lords of the Ring
programme.desc: Find out which joker always brings a bag of dental hygiene products to set, why Q changed into cargo pants in a store window and where the art department found the turquoise ring.

programme.title: Chris Webber's Full Court Pranks: Chris Webber vs. Bo the Fan
programme.desc: Broadcasting legend Rich Eisen shows one fan there's no such thing as a free lunch, an interactive video game gets a little too realistic, and Chris Webber teams up with former teammate Lou Williams to get things popping.

programme.title: Chris Webber's Full Court Pranks: Connor Fields vs. Taina the Fan
programme.desc: Josh Norman goes undercover again to talk about how much he hates Josh Norman, and one fan gets a wild ride with BMX legend Connor Fields.

programme.title: Chris Webber's Full Court Pranks: Tara Lipinski vs. Valeria the Fan
programme.desc: A pair of NASCAR enthusiasts get driven crazy when superstar driver Kyle Larsen surprises them with the ride of a lifetime, and skating legend Tara Lipinski recruits her biggest fan to be a whistle blower.

programme.title: Chris Webber's Full Court Pranks: Candace Parker vs. Delmy the Fan

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Washed Up
programme.desc: Q, Joe, Sal and Murr rat each other out at a pizza place and get into textual relationships with strangers at the mall. Then, the big loser twerks hard for the money at an impromptu bikini car wash.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Hump Day
programme.desc: The guys geek out while lending a hand to customers at a casual restaurant, then battle across the table of a focus group. Plus, tonight's big loser gets spitting mad when forced to spend some quality time at the zoo.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Autograph Corrector
programme.desc: The guys rely on the kindness of strangers to help answer some awkward questions, then go looking for love in the park. Plus, tonight's big loser strikes out amongst baseball fans with an assist from Yankees legend Tino Martinez.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Antisocial Network
programme.desc: Sal, Murr, Joe and Q play matchmakers at the food court, then switch fields to pose as architects, sharing some perplexing new building technology. Plus, tonight's big loser makes a connection while networking, and won't let go.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Lords of the Ring
programme.desc: The guys skirt the law while posing as receptionists, then go fishing at an unlikely locale. And the heat is on tonight's big losers as they try to give one unlucky customer the deal of a lifetime.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Running of the Bullies
programme.desc: Q, Murr, Joe and Sal offer up peculiar specials at a steakhouse and get creepy while helping to clean a prop house. Plus, tonight's big loser realizes he may need his own lifeguard after making waves at the pool.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Sal, Murr, Joe and Q pull desk duty, and then do strangers a solid by barring them from the bathroom. Plus, the big loser learns the hard way that gratuity is not included.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Rock Botton
programme.desc: The guys 'do the farts' with actor Jeff Daniels, and then try to get strangers to take their sides in ridiculous arguments. Later, tonight's loser finds himself up against a wall around a group of college students.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Easy Come, Easy Go
programme.desc: Charlotte prompts Trey to propose; Big encounters Carrie and Aidan at a furniture show.

programme.title: Sex and the City: All or Nothing
programme.desc: Carrie's fling weighs on her conscience; Charlotte plays hardball over a pre-nup.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Running With Scissors
programme.desc: Carrie seeks advice about Big; Samantha's new guy wants her to be tested for AIDS.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Don't Ask, Don't Tell
programme.desc: On the eve of her wedding, Charlotte admits that she and Trey have not slept together.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Escape From New York
programme.desc: While visiting L.A., Samantha meets a model and Carrie meets Matthew McConaughey.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Sex and Another City
programme.desc: While in Los Angeles, the girls head to the Playboy Mansion for a pool party.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Hot Child in the City
programme.desc: Charlotte tackles Trey's problem, while Samantha throws a party for a mature 13-year-old.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Frenemies
programme.desc: Miranda is shocked to learn that her date has died; Carrie teaches a seminar.

programme.title: Sex and the City: What Goes Around Comes Around
programme.desc: Carrie apologizes to Natasha, and Charlotte encounters a gardener at the McDougal estate.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Cock a Doodle Do!
programme.desc: When Big invites Carrie to lunch, she ignores Miranda's advice and accepts his invitation.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Agony and the Ex-Tacy
programme.desc: Carrie is left alone at a restaurant on her birthday and ponders the idea of soulmates.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Real Me
programme.desc: Carrie is asked to join a fashion show that features both models and 'real people.'

programme.title: Sex and the City: Defining Moments
programme.desc: Carrie meets a jazzman to help her get over Big; Samantha crosses some boundaries.

programme.title: Sex and the City: What's Sex Got to Do With It?
programme.desc: Samantha actually begins to date someone that she likes; Miranda opts for celibacy.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Ghost Town
programme.desc: Charlotte's battle with her mother-in-law explodes; Samantha's relationship gets a boost.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Baby, Talk Is Cheap
programme.desc: Carrie is still crazy about Aidan and wants him back; Charlotte rethinks her baby plans.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Time and Punishment
programme.desc: Carrie frets about Aidan's ability to forgive her affair; Miranda discovers leisure.

programme.title: Sex and the City: My Motherboard, Myself
programme.desc: Carrie and Aidan exchange keys; Miranda strains to maintain her strong woman image.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Sex and the Country
programme.desc: Samantha joins Carrie at Aidan's cottage in the country where she meets a cute farmer.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Belles of the Balls
programme.desc: Carrie helps Aidan and Big learn to accept each other, while Miranda and Steve get closer.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Coulda, Woulda, Shoulda
programme.desc: Miranda reveals that she's pregnant, and after much deliberation, she decides to have the baby. Meanwhile, Samantha uses her connection as a representative for Lucy Liu to her advantage so she can nab a great handbag.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Just Say Yes
programme.desc: Carrie is nervous when Aidan suggests that they move in together. To make matters worse, she finds an engagement ring among his things and questions whether he is 'the one.' Miranda and Steve decide to raise the baby together, while living separately.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Once Aidan moves in to her apartment, Carrie feels crowded. Meanwhile, Samantha is unnerved when she realizes she is falling in love with her boss. Elsewhere, Miranda is worried that having sex will hurt the baby.

programme.title: Sex and the City: All That Glitters
programme.desc: Carrie befriends a gay guy but she soon realizes that she has to fight for his attention when they go out together. While under the influence of ecstasy, Samantha confesses to Richard that she loves him. Miranda's co-worker tells everyone she is pregnant.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Change of a Dress
programme.desc: When Carrie tells Aidan she wants to take things slower, he refuses to give her time and moves out of their apartment the following day. Meanwhile, the monogamy bug bites Samantha when she finds out Richard is sleeping with other women.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Ring a Ding Ding
programme.desc: Carrie has a serious financial problem; Miranda and Steve grapple with raising a baby.

programme.title: Sex and the City: A Vogue Idea
programme.desc: Carrie begins her new job at Vogue; Charlotte hosts Miranda's baby shower.

programme.title: Sex and the City: I Heart NY
programme.desc: Carrie becomes nostalgic about her relationship with Mr. Big and the city she loves.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Anchors Away
programme.desc: The girls try to come to terms with Miranda's new life with Brady; Carrie meets a sailor.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Unoriginal Sin
programme.desc: Samantha gets back together with Richard; Carrie has a dry spell in her love life.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Luck Be an Old Lady
programme.desc: The girls head to Atlantic City for a 'girls' weekend; Samantha can't trust Richard.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Cover Girl
programme.desc: Samantha offers to help Carrie with her book cover; Charlotte shops on-line.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Plus One Is the Loneliest Number
programme.desc: Samantha organizes Carrie's book-launch party; Miranda doesn't tell her date about Brady.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Carrie obsesses over a comment made about Aidan; Samantha baby-sits Brady.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Big Journey
programme.desc: Hoping to see Big, Carrie travels to a book reading in San Francisco with Samantha.

programme.title: Sex and the City: I Love a Charade
programme.desc: The girls head to the Hamptons for the memorable wedding of a supposedly gay singer.

programme.title: Sex and the City: To Market, to Market
programme.desc: Carrie calms her nerves over a first date with a fellow author by going on another date.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Great Sexpectations
programme.desc: Carrie is disappointed by Jack's lacklustre performance between the sheets.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Perfect Present
programme.desc: Carrie spends a sleepless night at Berger's apartment; Samantha meets her waiter again.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Pick a Little, Talk a Little
programme.desc: Berger reacts poorly to Carrie's criticism; Charlotte and Harry have an argument.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Lights, Camera, Relationship
programme.desc: Carrie's success makes Berger uncomfortable; Samantha adds buzz for Smith's play.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Hop, Skip and a Week
programme.desc: Charlotte is set up by matchmakers at her synagogue; Carrie and Berger take a break.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Post-It Always Sticks Twice
programme.desc: The girls have a night out to help Carrie recover from Berger's break-up message.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Catch
programme.desc: Charlotte and Harry's wedding day arrives and is filled with unexpected mishaps.

programme.title: Sex and the City: A Woman's Right to Shoes
programme.desc: Carrie's expensive new shoes go missing when she attends a friend's baby shower.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Boy, Interrupted
programme.desc: Carrie reunites with her high school boyfriend; Samantha sneaks into an exclusive club.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Domino Effect
programme.desc: Big returns to New York for surgery; Miranda introduces Steve to her new love interest.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The One
programme.desc: Carrie is pursued by a famed Russian artist while Miranda admits her feelings to Steve.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Takeout: Funky Finds in 'da House
programme.desc: Guy and his son video chat with four DD alums that walk them through one of their recipes. The recipes include a funky waffle from Syracuse, gourmet mac 'n' cheese from Cleveland and California smoked turkey and green chile pork with an island twist.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: From Port to Port
programme.desc: Guy's going from Portsmouth, New Hampshire to Portland, Ore. for righteous coast-to-coast flavor. In Oregon, a Spanish spot serving outrageous octopus and an homage to the chef's childhood.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Family Meals
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is embracing family-run spots on this trip. In Ottawa, Canada, two brothers-in-law carryin' on a decades-long seafood tradition. In Richmond, Va., a mother-son duo running a breakfast, lunch and dinner joint in honor of their Pop.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Food Done Right
programme.desc: Guy's diggin' into food made the right way, including clam cakes and cheesefries.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Kings and Queens Of 'Cue
programme.desc: Guy's got the line on some killer barbecue. In Compton, Calif., the backyard joint cuttin' up ribs and real deal collard greens.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Southern to South American
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri digs into Southern comfort food and South American specialties. In Atlanta, a real-deal soul food spot dishing out collard green lasagna and a twist on fried chicken.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Meat Lover's Paradise
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's signing up for a meat-crazed master class. In Portland, Ore., the craft-cooking family joint with a chef who butchers, cures, preserves and smokes everything in-house.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Pork, Pasta and Barbecue
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's checking out some tried and true local faves. In Kansas City, Mo., the neighbourhood favourite killin' it with their scratch-made standouts like pork hash with black beans.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A Festival of Flavor
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's pullin' over for the bounty of the county.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Loaded, Stuffed and Fried
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's hitting the road and digging into joints that are loading it, stuffing it and frying it. In Santa Fe, N.M., a farm-to-table gem putting out hearty goodness like bison gravy fries and a lights-out lamb sandwich.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Takeout: Tastier at Home
programme.desc: Four chefs walk Guy virtually through one of their dishes. In this episode, there's a French dip from Seattle, San Diego arancini, Boston kabobs and bangin' shrimp all the way from North Pole, Alaska.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Hot Meat and Cool Veg
programme.desc: Modesto, Calif. has Portuguese sausage and lamb burgers. Oakland has a vegan joint with okra gumbo. Also, wings and pork rinds in Islamorada, Fla.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Kickin' It in Key West
programme.desc: Guy's checking out some of the awesome eats of Key West, Fla. From a funky little shack serving up New England seafood with coastal twists to a husband and wife team dishing up standout fish tacos.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Cannoli, Fritters and Wings
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's rolling out for a festival of flavours. In Portland, Ore., the Guamanian food truck is serving up righteous shrimp fritters and a unique spin on chicken salad.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A Little Twisted
programme.desc: Guy sets out to try the unexpected, be it an old fast food joint in New York with secret Italian family recipes or a Japanese grill/ dinner in California.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Old Time Attitude
programme.desc: Guy explores a diner that serves an old-school Southern classic: meat and three sides.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Bringin' It Home
programme.desc: Guy visits a local joint in California to try homemade Tuscan-style Italian food.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Gone Global
programme.desc: Guy tries a Venezuelan sandwich loaded with shredded beef, black beans, and cheese.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Surf 'n Turf
programme.desc: Guy checks out his own take on surf and surf in Mayport, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Burgers, Tacos and Dogs
programme.desc: Guy samples great food in Houston, TX and a joint in a grocery store offers wild toppings.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Who'Da Thunk It?
programme.desc: Guy visits a 50 year-old drive-in in Boise, Idaho where you can order prime rib. Then, a pizza joint in Sante Fe, N.M. where they offer green chillies as a topping. Plus, Jamaican cooking in Fort Worth, Texas.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Family Favorites
programme.desc: Guy Fieri takes a seat at the family table; In Glendale, Ariz., a German restaurant making sauerbraten from scratch; In Northern California, an old saloon serving lamb shanks; And in Fort Worth, a Texas institution dishing up all the classics.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Giada Entertains: Tuscan Barbecue Party
programme.desc: Giada celebrates her recent travels to Italy with a dinner party featuring the flavors of Tuscany. Giada creates a California barbecue vibe with an Italian touch with her menu featuring grilled chicken involtini, and buttery herbed potatoes.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Barbecue in the Big Apple
programme.desc: Bobby Flay grabs a meal from his favorite New York City barbecue joint, Mighty Quinn's, then makes a delicious brunch with all the leftovers. He whips up some BBQ Pulled Pork Corn Chip Pie and other dishes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Cook Like a Pro: Tomatoes
programme.desc: It's all about tomatoes, one of Ina's favourite ingredients. They are starring in her Easy Provental Lamb that has 100 % wow factor and in over the top good Penne Arrabiata, the spiciest pasta in town.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Patio Party: A Dip for Everyone
programme.desc: Valerie hosts a summer get-together. The menu includes grilled vegetables with avocado yogurt dip and tapenade aioli dip.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Easy Entertaining
programme.desc: Ree hosts a cooking and eating get-together with her Oklahoma City Thunder friends. The menu includes white spinach artichoke lasagna and a butternut kale salad.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Cowboy Drive-Through
programme.desc: Ree serves up sensational packed lunches at the Drummond drive-through. She makes ranch hand sandwiches with pop pulled pork and fixins, spicy sweet potato chips, a cowboy fruit salad and triple-chocolate cow pies.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: 16-Minute Summertime
programme.desc: Ree makes big bad beef skewers with green and blue cheese mayo, and shrimp and pineapple flatbreads with avocado cream.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Home Sweet Home 1
programme.desc: Ree and the kids shoot a fun show with lots of hijinks and an Italian vibe. They make orecchiette with sausage and kale for past pronto and luscious leftovers star in a crazy mashup of pepperoni fried rice.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Midwest Classics
programme.desc: Molly is sharing her most prized Midwest recipes. They're classic and comforting with the signature Yeh twist!

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Take Me Out to the Ball Game
programme.desc: Valerie's always been a football fan, but today Valerie's longtime friend, Lisa, and her husband, former baseball player Billy Ashley, are going to show Valerie how much fun baseball culture can be.

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: Backyard Grill
programme.desc: Kardea fires up the grill for an old-fashioned backyard barbecue and invites her family over to enjoy some of her charbroiled dishes. She makes pimento cheese-stuffed burgers and grilled sea island wings, plus southern pickles and okra on the side.

programme.title: Guy's Ranch Kitchen: Cocktail Hour
programme.desc: Instead of a killer dinner, Guy Fieri invites his chef friends to make finger foods, hors d'oeuvres and appetizers for cocktail hour at the ranch. Antonia Lofaso prepares cones of tender skirt steak with a chimichurri sauce and potato frites.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Spring Into Summer
programme.desc: The Kitchen is serving a week's worth of easy meals perfect for warmer weather, like Katie Lee's Mediterranean grilled chicken and eggplant and Jeff Mauro's Thai meatballs and zucchini noodles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: World Class Flavor
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's crossing the country to get a taste of world-class flavour. From California to Florida and Seattle to Massachusetts, he's divin' into classic Greek, legit pierogis, and real-deal Belgian waffles made European-style.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple-D All-Stars
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's in the kitchen with some real Triple-D all-stars, cookin' up everything from gangster Greek in Salt Lake City to creative curry in Denver to amazing Asian in Miami and North Pole, Alaska.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Best of the Beach
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's going coastal for the best eats on the beach. He's reelin' in everything from the freshest catch in Florida and Mexico, to Hawaiian open-air barbecue and a taste of Cuba in Miami.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Justice League's Next Top Talent Idol Star
programme.desc: A comedic look at the superheroes’ lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens when their capes are left at home.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Justice League’s Next Top Talent Idol Star: Second Greatest Team Edition
programme.desc: The Justice League holds another talent competition to find the second-best superhero team in the world. The competition heats up and the Titans must find a way to beat the Hive Five.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Bro-pocalypse
programme.desc: When the male Titans are injured, Raven and Starfire must become bros to stop the Bropocalypse.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: The Scoop
programme.desc: Starfire befriends an ice cream scoop and steals it from the HIVE's ice cream social. The rest of the titans show concern over her love of inanimate objects.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Flashback
programme.desc: A flashback to how the Teen Titans first formed.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Kabooms
programme.desc: The Titans go to see the film adaptation of their favourite show 'Babies and Dogs'. Inspired by the movie they decide to make a Hollywood film of their own.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Chicken in the Cradle
programme.desc: Beast Boy lays an egg, but instead of taking care of his newly-hatched chick he goofs off and lets the others deal with it.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Tower Renovation
programme.desc: The Titans after 'Teen Titans Go! To the Movies'.

programme.title: American Dad!: The Missing Kink
programme.desc: Francine does all she can to make Stan take on a different mission. And when he does, she learns that her missionary man is all she really needs.

programme.title: American Dad!: Max Jets

programme.desc: When Stewie's new best friend falls ill, Lois takes him to the hospital where she learns that he has a critical, but treatable, disease. The boy's parents, however, refuse him treatment due to their religious beliefs.

programme.title: My Hero Academia: Deku vs. Gentle Criminal

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: I Used to Be a Peoples

programme.desc: Starfire dreams of becoming a successful business alien princess and joins a cookie-selling organization to learn the proper skills.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: The Metric System vs. Freedom

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: The Chaff
programme.desc: Control Freak shows some deleted scenes in an effort to get Teen Titans Go! canceled.

programme.desc: After receiving a galactic crime alert the Titans take off for an epic space adventure. The Titans have to stop Darkseid's latest scheme but the excitement of the space adventure might be too much for Robin to handle.

programme.desc: A comedic look at the superheroes’ lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens when their capes are left at home.

programme.desc: Robin's master detective skills help him to find out what happened to the animals that live beyond Titans Tower.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: The Avogodo
programme.desc: It's Cinco de Mayo and Robin learns that avocados are a super food that give super powers.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Orangins
programme.desc: The Titans tell their slightly inaccurate origin stories.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Jinxed
programme.desc: A game of jinx doesn't work out for Robin when he ends up losing his voice.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!: Beast Boy's That's What's Up
programme.desc: Beast Boy goes to visit the Doom Patrol. Beast Boy, Negative Girl, and Robotman discover some crabs are up to no good.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Twenty-Five Little Pre-Pubers Without a Snootful
programme.desc: When Alan and Judith argue during a rehearsal for Jake's play, Charlie is stuck handling a roomful of fourth graders by himself. Charlie then uses his jingle-writing experience to craft the play -- with an interesting outcome.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Yes, Monsignor
programme.desc: Charlie's ex-flame returns to his life with a little more baggage than when she left.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Carpet Burns and a Bite Mark
programme.desc: Charlie organizes an intervention in order to keep Alan from reuniting with his ex-wife, Judith.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Your Dismissive Attitude Toward Boobs
programme.desc: Charlie and Alan get into a power struggle when Alan allows Berta to stay at Charlie's house for a few days while her brother tries to wean himself off drugs at her place. Berta, who has an ulterior motive, plays one brother against the other.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Something Salted and Twisted
programme.desc: Alan has a family dinner to celebrate the publication of a newspaper article that calls him a 'good doctor, neighbour and guy,' but he's crushed when his mother is not impressed. After drowning his sorrow in many drinks, he has a life-changing epiphany.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Humiliation Is a Visual Medium
programme.desc: No one believes Charlie when he says that his relationship with Mia is getting serious, and everyone really thinks he's lost it when he confesses that they have been dating for more than a month and haven't consummated the relationship.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Love Isn't Blind, It's Retarded
programme.desc: Charlie plans a romantic date with Mia in the hopes of finally consummating their relationship. Meanwhile, Alan starts dating Kandi, Charlie's most recent ex-girlfriend, who is half his age.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: The Spit-Covered Cobbler
programme.desc: When Kandi's old car breaks down, Alan ends up leasing her a new one. Charlie warns him to break things off with her, but when Alan spends his last dime on Kandi's root canal and can't pay Judith's alimony, Charlie is forced to cover for him.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Golly Moses, She's a Muffin
programme.desc: Charlie's not happy when he learns that Kandi has been living in sin with Alan in his house, particularly because of his own recent dry spell with women. Things begin to look up for Charlie when a special visitor comes knocking on his door.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Always a Bridesmaid, Never a Burro
programme.desc: Alan is depressed that his hot, young girlfriend, Kandi, has left him to go stay with his bitter ex-wife, Judith, who has brainwashed her against him. Meanwhile, Kandi sets Judith up on a date with her father, Andy.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: And the Plot Moistens
programme.desc: Jake's teacher, Francine, calls Alan and Judith in to discuss Jake's lack of interest in school. Desperate to communicate with someone intelligent while continuing to see his airhead girlfriend, Kandi, Alan starts a platonic relationship with Francine.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Just Once With Aunt Sophie
programme.desc: When a cute girl from Jake's school invites him to a party, Charlie butts in on Alan's milestone moment with his son by offering his own words of wisdom on women.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: If My Hole Could Talk
programme.desc: Charlie finds himself attracted to an older woman who writes self-help books. Alan and Jake battle over a school assignment.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Waiting for the Right Snapper
programme.desc: Charlie tries to resist temptation when he meets the grown daughter of the woman he's dating, and then realizes they've met before.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Taterhead Is Our Love Child
programme.desc: Charlie encounters an ex-girlfriend whose son looks an awful lot like him.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: A Jock Strap in Hell
programme.desc: The guys bump into Jake's former teacher who became a stripper after a fling with Charlie. Charlie tries to help her out by giving her a room and a job, but things spiral out of control.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: It's Always Nazi Week
programme.desc: When Judith kicks Herb to the curb, Alan comes knocking.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Best H.O. Money Can Buy
programme.desc: Alan tries to escape Judith's grasp as Charlie and Herb go on the prowl.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Pinocchio's Mouth
programme.desc: Charlie's new girlfriend asks him to spend the night at her place while Alan tries to enforce a punishment on Jake.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: This Is Not Gonna End Well
programme.desc: Jake becomes Charlie's designated driver after he has his license suspended and to Charlie's chagrin, Alan makes plans to attend Chelsea's birthday party.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Exile on Lame Street
programme.desc: Fearing that Ritchie is too young to be exposed to sex, drugs and rock and roll vices on display at a Rolling Stones concert, Christine initially forbids it. Eventually, she relents but immediately regrets and in a panic, goes to the venue to keep tabs.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Some of My Best Friends Are Portuguese
programme.desc: Shut out once again by the judgemental moms at Ritchie's school, Christine runs into a down-to-earth workingwoman, who invites her over for a drink after work. Belinda's job, however, isn't quite the type of position Christine expected her to hold.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: A Fair to Remember
programme.desc: Christine volunteers to help out at Richie's spring carnival and while there, she runs into the mean moms, who are clucking over the attractive father of a new student at Westbridge. Christine is excited to learn that the hot dad is her ex-boyfriend.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: The Passion of the Christine
programme.desc: Since New Christine broke up with Richard, he's been spending a lot of time at Christine's house, leading Ritchie to believe that his parents are back together. Richard suggests that they give their relationship another try.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: The Answer Is Maybe
programme.desc: Richard tells Christine that the reason he has good luck with women is that he 'says yes to life.' When Christine meets a good-looking man after paying a visit to New Christine, she breaks out of her mold and says yes when he asks her out to dinner.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Come to Papa Jeff
programme.desc: When Christine's parents can't make it to Grandparent's Day at Ritchie's school, New Christine suggests that Jeff, the new man in Christine's life, go as Ritchie's special friend. Christine then learns that Jeff really is a grandfather.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: End of the Curse
programme.desc: Blanche is mortified after taking a pregnancy test which indicates she's pregnant. Then after visiting her doctor, she learns the truth, she's beginning menopause.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Ladies of the Evening
programme.desc: The house is fumigated and the girls move into a Miami Beach hotel. Previously, Blanche had won three tickets to see the opening of Burt Reynolds new movie and passes to the party held afterwards where they could meet him.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Take Him, He's Mine
programme.desc: Dorothy dumps a depressed Stan on Blanche so that she can go out with a naval officer.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: It's a Miserable Life
programme.desc: The girls petition to save an old oak tree on the property of a hateful woman.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Isn't it Romantic
programme.desc: Dorothy's old friend, Jean comes to Miami for a visit and Dorothy decides not to tell Blanche and Rose that Jean is a lesbian. Then, after spending time with Rose, Jean begins to fall for her.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Big Daddy's Little Lady
programme.desc: Blanche's dad announces he's remarrying; Rose and Dorothy enter a song-writing contest.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Family Affair
programme.desc: A generational clash occurs when Dorothy's son Michael and Rose's daughter Bridget visit.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Vacation
programme.desc: The girls' dream vacation in the Caribbean turns out to be far from what was advertised in the brochure. While the girls are away, Sophia romances the gardener.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Joust Between Friends
programme.desc: Laid off from work, Dorothy searches for part-time work which leads to Blanche getting her a job at the museum. Then fur flies when Dorothy is handed the job of organizing a banquet, the duty Blanche was hoping for.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Love, Rose
programme.desc: Rose places an ad in the personals column and she is let down when she doesn't hear an immediate response. This leads Blanche and Dorothy to begin responding under the name Isaac Newton.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Sisters
programme.desc: As a birthday present for Sophia, Dorothy brings Sophia's sister over from Sicily.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Stan Who Came to Dinner
programme.desc: Dorothy feels sympathy for Stan when he tells her he's going to have open heart surgery. However that sympathy fades when she learns of his many infidelities while they were married.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Actor
programme.desc: The girls vie for the attention of a famous TV actor when he stars in a local play.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Before and After
programme.desc: Rose is convinced she died during a throat spasm. This leads her to live her life to the hilt, totally disrupting the other girls' lives. Then, when the girls complain about her new lifestyle, Rose moves out.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: And Then There Was One
programme.desc: Sophia readies herself for participating in a walkathon. Meanwhile, Dorothy, Blanche, and Rose babysit for the walkathon participants' children. But when the event is over, a baby is left unclaimed.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Bedtime Story
programme.desc: When the girls learn that a number of relatives are going to be visiting, they reminisce about times they've had to share a bed and strange places they've had to sleep, including a train station.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Forgive Me, Father
programme.desc: Blanche and Rose plan a nice dinner at the house for Dorothy and her new boyfriend, who she later learns is a priest. She later gets another shock when she learns he plans to leave the church.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Long Day's Journey Into Marinara
programme.desc: Sophia's sister, Angela arrives in Miami for a visit and decides to move there but while she's looking for an apartment Dorothy invites her to stay with the girls. This ticks off Sophia since she thinks that Angela is after everything that she has.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Whose Face Is This, Anyway?
programme.desc: Blanche is convinced that her beauty is fading after attending her high school reunion and discovers many of her classmates have had face lifts. Meanwhile, Rose is making a home movie for class she's taking.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Dorothy's Prized Pupil
programme.desc: Dorothy feels that it's her fault when her prized pupil wins a writing contest which inadvertently signals the INS that he’s an illegal alien.

programme.title: The Zoo: A New Beginning
programme.desc: Max the camel faces new challenges as he ages. The zoo prepares to give an injured turkey vulture a permanent home, but after healing, there may be a change of plan. Willow the red panda gives birth to two baby cubs.

programme.title: The Zoo: Mending Willow
programme.desc: One team tests the problem solving skills of lions against those of snow leopards. Willow, a young red panda, is having mysterious foot problems. Also, the bird department anxiously awaits the gender reveal of two new adjutant stork chicks.

programme.title: The Zoo: Bronx Tales: Velcro the Incredible
programme.desc: An okapi called Magani is ready to breed and is paired with veteran Kweli to learn the ropes. Lola the blue iguana plays hard-to-get when introduced to a potential mate, and anteater Velcro shows off her talents with a new tool.

programme.title: The Zoo: Fawns Become Friends
programme.desc: Two sickly newborn fawns form an unbreakable bond while they are hand-reared by staff. Leopard Khazo gets a surprise that enhances her life, and tensions run high as the reptile staff attempts to collect blood from Cuban crocs.

programme.title: Saved By the Barn: Baby Fever
programme.desc: The barn partners with Michigan Humane Society to find forever homes for four neglected calves. As night falls, they welcome a litter of piglets, and Chris is tasked to build a playground for the goats.

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego - What's New at the Zoo?: Welcome to Our World
programme.desc: The condor team takes extreme measures to save this highly endangered species, while a klipspringer doe is welcomed and must be nursed back to health.

programme.title: The Zoo: Back to Africa
programme.desc: Zoo staff make the journey to east Africa to return to the wild thousands of toads they helped rescue from extinction. Utu, the test-pilot giraffe, tries out a new piece of equipment. And the bird department works to save the lives of millions of birds.

programme.title: Saved By the Barn: Baby Fever
programme.desc: The barn partners with Michigan Humane Society to find forever homes for four neglected calves. As night falls, they welcome a litter of piglets, and Chris is tasked to build a playground for the goats.

programme.title: The Zoo: Fawns Become Friends
programme.desc: Two sickly newborn fawns form an unbreakable bond while they are hand-reared by staff. Leopard Khazo gets a surprise that enhances her life, and tensions run high as the reptile staff attempts to collect blood from Cuban crocs.

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego: Welcome to Our World
programme.desc: A baby klipspringer is born on one of the coldest nights of the season and is miraculously nursed back to health. The California condor team at the Safari Park goes to unbelievable lengths to save this highly endangered species.

programme.title: The Zoo: Birds and the Bees
programme.desc: Leo, a snow leopard rescued from Pakistan, is having trouble eating because of a broken canine. The bird department works feverishly to restore a beehive for the bee-eater birds. Ambassador Center staff work with an adorable fennec fox named Charlie.

programme.title: The Zoo: Fawns Become Friends
programme.desc: Two sickly newborn fawns form an unbreakable bond while they are hand-reared by staff. Leopard Khazo gets a surprise that enhances her life, and tensions run high as the reptile staff attempts to collect blood from Cuban crocs.

programme.title: Saved By the Barn: Baby Fever
programme.desc: The barn partners with Michigan Humane Society to find forever homes for four neglected calves. As night falls, they welcome a litter of piglets, and Chris is tasked to build a playground for the goats.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Boat Load of Trouble
programme.desc: Maine Game Wardens head-out on jet skis and speed boats to patrol the rivers and lakes.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Harvest Time
programme.desc: The Wardens split their time patrolling the water and the woods.

programme.title: North Woods Law: The Rookies
programme.desc: The warden's come across a family with a surprising collection of weapons in their car.

programme.title: North Woods Law: And the Little Ones Too
programme.desc: The discovery of a dead beaver in a Skowhegan parking lot leads Warden Parker on an exhaustive investigation involving surveillance cameras, anonymous informants and the assistance of the local police.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Dirty Habits
programme.desc: Parker investigates a rotting bear carcass that was dumped on the side of the road, leading him on a hunt across Maine to find a potential poacher. Blanchard catches a couple behaving badly on a wildlife refuge. Robertson rescues some ducklings.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Catcher in the Wild
programme.desc: A speeding dirt biker eludes Warden Robertson, but leaves a crucial clue to his whereabouts. Two dogs on the loose are chasing newborn deer. An ATV accident victim has an odd explanation for what caused his crash.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Weed Whackers
programme.desc: Officer Arsenault teams up with police to stake-out a marijuana grow in the middle of the woods.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Under the Radar
programme.desc: Officer Canfield breaks up a 45-year-old illegal fishing tournament. Officer MacFadzen rescues an injured hawk. Officer Hannett confronts two kids riding OHRVs on the road. On Mt. Chocorua, officers rescue a woman with a fractured ankle.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Fatal Attraction
programme.desc: Lt. Bill Boudreau investigates a complaint about a man illegally feeding bears. Officer Jon Demler pulls over a swerving car, only to find himself in the middle of a domestic dispute. The dive team searches for a man who may have drowned in Kingston.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Decoy Detail
programme.desc: Officer Egan sets up a decoy and checks hunters at a popular bird hunting site. Officers Towne and Bronson help rescue an injured hiker on Mount Kearsarge. Plus, Lt. Boudreau and Officer MacFadzen investigate a hunting on private property call.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Triple Threat
programme.desc: Up north, Sgt. Lucas checks duck hunters. Down south, Officer Benvenuti and Trainee Crouse question a man suspected of hunting over bait. Later, Sgt. Lucas and Officer Holmes attempt to free a cub stuck inside a trap before its mother returns.

programme.title: BET Awards
programme.desc: Watch the star-studded awards show.

programme.title: Movie: Blue Streak
programme.desc: A jewel thief is released from jail and sets out to find his hidden loot. However, when he returns to his hiding spot he finds the area has been turned into a police station and his only shot of recovering the diamonds is to impersonate a detective.

programme.title: Movie: Welcome Home, Roscoe Jenkins
programme.desc: A successful Los Angeles talk show host heads home to the Deep South to reunite with his family. Once there, he learns how easy it is fall back into old rivalries.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Garage Tales/ Animal Farm
programme.desc: After finding a box of mementos, Gramma tells the kids the story of her greatest adventure. While the Greens are away, the animals compete to see who will rule the farm.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Space Chicken/ Steak Night
programme.desc: To impress his new neighbors, Cricket attempts to launch a chicken into space. The Greens are excited to continue their traditional monthly steak dinner, but Cricket accidentally loses the steaks on the subway.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Cricket Versus/ Blue Tater
programme.desc: Cricket must wrestle a wild animal in order to prove himself worthy of the Green family name. When Cricket persuades Bill to convert his pickup into a French fry food truck, he discovers a mysterious blue potato that Gramma says is cursed with bad luck.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Swimming Fool/ Tilly's Goat
programme.desc: When the Greens visit an indoor pool, Cricket is challenged to jump off the high-dive. Tilly claims that their goat has the spirit of a dog and enters it in a dog show.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Gramma's License/ Bear Trapped
programme.desc: When Gramma crashes into a parked police car, she loses her driver’s license and her confidence. Cricket Green hates bears.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Gridlocked/ Mama Bird
programme.desc: Bill brings the family out to the country for a road trip, but a traffic jam halts their plans. Tilly finds a bird's nest and tries to raise the chicks as her own, but they choose Cricket.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Cricketsitter/ Backflip Bill
programme.desc: Bill takes Gramma to a doctor’s checkup and leaves Tilly in charge of Cricket. Cricket learns that Bill never realized his childhood dream of becoming a gymnast.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Welcome Home/ Raccooned
programme.desc: Cricket Green and his family move from the country to Big City to live with Gramma. Cricket and Bill accidentally disturb some raccoons in the garage, and they invade the house!

programme.title: Big City Greens: Breaking News / Cyberbullies
programme.desc: When Bill grows a huge watermelon, Cricket invites the local news to see the mega melon. Cricket stands up to the cyberbullying of three online gamers known as the Cyber Knights.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Fill Bill/ Critterball Crisis
programme.desc: When the Greens dine at a seafood restaurant, Cricket and Tilly decide to rescue a live octopus.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Parade Day/ DIY Guys
programme.desc: Cricket leaves Gloria to work the coffee shop alone while he enjoys the parade on his break.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Tilly Tour/ Dinner Party
programme.desc: Tilly takes Gramma out to be a tourist for a day. Cricket and Remy arrange a dinner party for everyone to get to know each other.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Barry Cuda/ Suite Retreat
programme.desc: Cricket uses his first tip from working at Big Coffee to buy a singing toy fish. Gramma kicks the family out of the house for the day.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Family Legacy/ Paint Misbehavin'
programme.desc: Gramma tells Cricket and Tilly that there’s treasure buried on their property.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Rated Cricket / Homeshare Hoedown

programme.title: See No Evil: All Eyes on Jordie
programme.desc: On April 10th 2016, Jordie Hudson vanishes after a night out with friends. The only clue police have is her glasses, which are discovered in a pool of blood. Surveillance footage shows a horrific attack.

programme.title: See No Evil: The Man in the Red Jacket
programme.desc: On Valentine's night, 2015, 27-year-old James Enright is stabbed to death outside a busy train station in Burnaby, Canada. Despite cell phone footage of the attack, police struggle to identify his killer, until CCTV leads them to an unlikely suspect.

programme.title: See No Evil: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: On November 7, 2014, 69-year old Ina Clare Richardson is found brutally murdered in a public park in Clarkston, Washington. With no witnesses to the crime, investigators turn to surveillance cameras to piece together the puzzle of her fateful final night.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Let Her Rot
programme.desc: When Joshua Hudnall went off to war, he felt more at home in combat than he ever did around his mother. Just when Joshua thought he had escaped her torment, she set her sights on someone else, and destroyed their family from within.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: My Son's Prisoner
programme.desc: Shelly Lietz had been afraid of her son ever since he was four years old. She knew she saw something evil in his eyes that scared her. As he got older and more violent, that fear crippled her, eventually making her a prisoner in her own home.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: He Said There Were More of Them
programme.desc: It never occurred to Shirley Gaskins that the father she adored could be one of the most prolific serial killers out there, but, as more people vanished without explanation, she was finding it harder to deny.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: I Have To Do The Right Thing
programme.desc: When Tim Kreider discovered proof that his son, Alec, was responsible for a horrific crime, he knew what the right thing to do was.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: I Tried to Prevent This
programme.desc: For 17 years, Kathy Swanson feared her son, Michael, would one day harm someone. For 17 years, she tried in vain to convince people that something about Michael was different until that dreadful day finally came that her worst nightmare came true.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: I Wish I'd Turned Around
programme.desc: Kat Harris and Marla Kreuger each loved Lawrence Harris in her own way. Each dismissed his dark obsession as childish and strange, but harmless. Neither of them realized he was preparing to sacrifice innocent lives for his evil purpose.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: He Was a Good Man to Me
programme.desc: When Alvin "Bud" Brown was arrested he told an officer that he was just like Hannibal Lecter. But, to his wife, Jackie, the man she shared a bed with for years appeared to be gentle and loving until the Thanksgiving Day when she discovered the truth.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: The Face of My Torturer
programme.desc: William Knorr's mother, Theresa, always threatened that if she ever went to jail for what she had done, she would take him down with her. After years spent helping her keep the horrors secret, could he ever escape or would he wind up like all the others?

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Monster in the Desert
programme.desc: In 1992, a 19-year-old woman is brutally assaulted outside of Desert Hot Springs, California. Miraculously she escapes, but lives in constant fear that he will find her again one day. What the victim doesn't realize is she survived a serial killer.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Children of Thunder
programme.desc: In the summer of 2000, panic overwhelms the Bay Area when bags full of body parts start appearing all over the region. Investigators follow the trail of grisly clues and it leads to the discovery of a secret cult carrying out a sinister scheme.

programme.title: Dark Waters: Murder in the Deep: Screams of the South Pacific
programme.desc: Serena Karlan leaves everything she knows behind to join her boyfriend, retired NBA champion Bison Dele, aboard his catamaran in the South Pacific. Serena is the happiest she's ever been, until Dele's estranged brother surfaces and throws them off course.

programme.title: Dark Waters: Murder in the Deep: Dead of Night
programme.desc: Malcolm and Lindy Robertson leave their family behind to sail the open seas of retirement together. Lindy eventually learns to love Malcolm's dream of sailing to Thailand but paradise soon turns into an unfathomable nightmare.

programme.title: Dark Waters: Murder in the Deep: Cursed Paradise
programme.desc: Two couples arrive on the same remote island in the Pacific looking for paradise. Soon, all that either of them finds is trouble. When one couple emerges months later, the FBI will spend years deciphering exactly what happened.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Night Shift Nightmare
programme.desc: The naked body of 19-year-old Wanda Musk is found just miles from where she worked at local gas station. In the wake of the vicious killing, suspicions mount over several young men in the community including her ex-boyfriend and a troubled local teen.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Trail of Frozen Tears
programme.desc: Pretty pre-teen Rosie Davenport is found brutally murdered in an abandoned house, the circumstances of her death eerily similar to the unsolved murder of 13-year-old Malissa Thomas one year earlier.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Killer Night Out
programme.desc: Bright and beautiful nurse's aide 19-year-old Janean Brown is found dead in a drainage ditch, her throat cut so deep she is almost decapitated. A riveting investigation will reveal a darker account of the night she died than anyone could have imagined.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Trail of Fear
programme.desc: The body of a 74-year-old woman is found bludgeoned to death, the gruesome discovery sends shockwaves across the community. Suspicion and paranoia take control. Then a young girl vanishes, and her case exposes sordid secrets hidden for decades.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Cabin Fever
programme.desc: After they fail to turn up for an annual maple tapping celebration, Ian and Nancy Blackburn's lifeless bodies are discovered stuff into the trunk of their own car. The whirlwind investigation will unmask a monster who hid in plain sight.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Deadly Fetish
programme.desc: When Debra Davis's husband accused her of being cold and immature for refusing to experiment sexually, she thought something was wrong with her. She never suspected her husband's perversions were signs that there was something evil deep within him.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: My Secret Nightmare
programme.desc: Tiffany Shore grew up knowing that her father was evil, but she still kept his secrets. When Tiffany finally escapes him, she is devastated to discover that has committed far more heinous acts than she ever imagined or knew about.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: In the Lion's Cage
programme.desc: Linda Wittmier and Terry Jo Howard both fell in love with James Michael Randall. And both suffered when they discovered the monster that lurked inside him. But they never realized how lucky they were to survive his twisted sexual desire to kill.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Vincent Gigante
programme.desc: Under the disguise of mental illness, Vincent Gigante evaded the authorities and rose to become the most powerful mobster in New York.

programme.title: The Mob's Assassin
programme.desc: As the head of Murder Inc. Albert Anastasia would oversee the American Mafia's most violent period in history. As Luciano's go-to killer, Albert and his hitmen committed murder wholesale.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Vito Genovese
programme.desc: The story of Vito Genovese, who ruthlessly dealt with disloyalty in the Mafia and quickly rose to prominence - but unwittingly caused his own downfall.

programme.title: John Gotti: The Teflon Don
programme.desc: The story of ambitious American mobster John Gotti, who became the boss of the Gambino family by ordering the death of the group's former head. He earned the nickname 'Teflon Don' after three acquittals.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Al Capone
programme.desc: America's first superstar gangster, Al Capone, dominated headlines and is said to be responsible for the bloody St. Valentines' Day Massacre. After being made the face of organized crime by U.S. government, Capone's public image brings about his downfall.

programme.title: Mafia's Day of Reckoning
programme.desc: What happened when the FBI pursued the five families who ran organised crime in New York, and the fate of two informers is revealed.

programme.title: Sam Giancana: Mafia vs. CIA
programme.desc: We reveal the secrets of the Mafia's most notorious members, beginning with Sam Giancana, who was associated with the Kennedys, Frank Sinatra, and the CIA.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Al Capone
programme.desc: America's first superstar gangster, Al Capone, dominated headlines and is said to be responsible for the bloody St. Valentines' Day Massacre. After being made the face of organized crime by U.S. government, Capone's public image brings about his downfall.

programme.title: John Gotti: The Teflon Don

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits: Vito Genovese
programme.desc: The story of Vito Genovese, who ruthlessly dealt with disloyalty in the Mafia and quickly rose to prominence - but unwittingly caused his own downfall.

programme.title: The Mob's Assassin
programme.desc: As the head of Murder Inc. Albert Anastasia would oversee the American Mafia's most violent period in history. As Luciano's go-to killer, Albert and his hitmen committed murder wholesale.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Designed for Power
programme.desc: Hitler's mastery of propaganda, and instinct for effective design, used to create everything from the swastika flag to the striking uniforms of the storm troopers, help win huge support for the Nazi party, a vital step in its march towards total power.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Hitler's Highways
programme.desc: Hitler promises to make Germany great again by creating 14,000 miles of freeway and building one and half million 'People's Cars' every year. Instead, the Nazi war economy relies on millions of forced labourers and prisoners.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: The Industry of War
programme.desc: Hitler is determined to rebuild Germany's military strength after the destruction of almost all its weaponry in the First World War. To overcome his country's dire shortage of raw materials, he plans to steal them from other countries.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: A Culture of Control
programme.desc: Hitler is determined to change the way German people think and feel by creating a new, Nazi form of art and culture. He takes over the film industry and radio stations, giving the Nazis complete control over every image and message his people receive.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Himmler's Empire of Terror
programme.desc: Hitler's bid for power depends on his most loyal, and violent supporters: the SS. Using surveillance, detention, and even murder, they defeat political enemies and are most responsible for putting Hitler's deadly policy of racial purity into practice.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Retreat from Reality
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler is determined to fight to the very end, even if it costs millions of Germans their lives. He puts his faith in his the 'Wunderwaffe' and the 'wonder weapons,' including the first ever ballistic missile, the V2.

programme.title: Weaponology: Bomber
programme.desc: In eight decades man went from the invention of powered flight to the B2 Stealth bomber.

programme.title: Weaponology: Fighter
programme.desc: Exploring the development of engine power, wings, maneuverability and ejection systems, find out how fighter jets now rule the land and sky.

programme.title: Weaponology: Armored Personnel Carriers
programme.desc: Trace the family trees of the greatest fighting vehicles and armoured personnel carriers in history.

programme.title: Somali Pirate Takedown: The Real Story
programme.desc: Find out the real story behind the Navy SEAL kill shots that finally freed Captain Richard Phillips.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Midway: Taking Back the Pacific
programme.desc: June 1942, six months after Pearl Harbour, the battle of Midway will change the course of history and the Pacific war. The Americans take on the most powerful navy in the world, and win.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Bottle Island
programme.desc: John Beal is ready to leave the city behind and embrace a life off the grid in Isla Mujeres, Mexico. He hires Richart Sowa and his son Eden to build an eco-friendly private floating island made from recycled plastic bottles.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Island Getaway
programme.desc: A family attempts to build a 400-square foot cabin situated on a small island in Northern Idaho. Access to the property is limited and rough waters make it difficult to bring materials in. It's a race to the finish as winter hits the island early.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Hawaiian Hideaway
programme.desc: Innovative designer and builder Graham Hill sets out to transform a steep Maui jungle plot into a 600-square-foot, hi-tech dream home with multiple moving walls and other space-maximizing features.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Not-So-Pleasant Camp On Pleasant Pond

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Past, Present and Future
programme.desc: A couple's Belle Island camp has a long multi-generational history within their family spanning nearly 100 years. Their camp is an ongoing focus of family life and they are now ready to make its preservation their main priority.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Ship-shape Shack
programme.desc: Chase and the team have their hands full transforming a family's treasured 1950s lobster shack into a cabin that will create a lifetime of memories for generations to come.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: A Cabin for the Bride
programme.desc: Chase and his team are back home in Augusta getting ready for Lance's wedding. Chase gets a call about a little log cabin stuck in a backyard. He sees the cabin as a fun project for the team to work on and present to Lance's lovely bride.

programme.desc: After years of visiting Emerald Isle, North Carolina, with their three kids, Matt and Megann are ready to buy a weekend getaway home at the beach. After finding a fixer-upper in their budget, they have $50,000 to create a luxurious outdoor space.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Full-on Fixer-Upper in St. Pete Beach, Florida
programme.desc: Steve and Stacie just moved to St. Pete Beach, Florida, and are looking to put down some roots. They find their perfect beachside home but it needs a ton of work.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Newlyweds Renovate Down the Shore
programme.desc: Newlyweds Jamie and Emily both grew up going to the Jersey Shore for family vacations. Now they're looking for a beachside bargain that they can renovate, but that might be tough to come by near the beaches of the Greater Atlantic City.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Living Through Renovations on North Padre Island, Texas

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Destined for a Renovation in Destin, Florida
programme.desc: Carrie and Lee have been splitting their time between Nashville and Destin, Florida but they want to get their two sons out of the city and into the sunshine - permanently.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Fripp Island Fix-Up
programme.desc: Jim and Sarah are looking for a diamond in the rough on the hidden gem of Fripp Island, North Carolina. They find a property with an unparalleled oceanfront overlook.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Run Down Family Rental
programme.desc: Ashley and Michael take on a rundown, family-owned 1939 craftsman. The project is an emotional journey for the duo as the bland home is transformed into a breath-taking abode with room expansions and some inspired flooring choices.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: The Kettle House
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley get the opportunity to renovate a bizarre Galveston icon that may harbor a few surprises inside. The spaceship-shaped landmark is soon brimming with life as the Cordrays bring their unique charm to the one-of-a-kind house.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Tiny Craftsman
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley revisit a house they tried to purchase a year ago and find the small 1920s craftsman has been stripped of all its original features.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Mystery House
programme.desc: Michael surprises Ashley with an 1893 raised cottage that he purchased before seeing the full interior. The duo soon finds themselves in over their heads when they realize it's not quite the hidden gem Michael thought it was.

programme.title: Good Eats: Reloaded: The Icing Man Cometh
programme.desc: Alton Brown finally 'reloads' this episode from season six with two new buttercreams, including one curious model flavored with buttermilk.

programme.title: Good Eats: Reloaded: The Egg Files
programme.desc: Crack the mysterious egg case and pursue a higher understanding of this amazing ovoid.
